question.text:
Q1. A water sample contains 0.002 g of lead (Pb²⁺) dissolved in 500 L of water.
Assuming the density of water = 1 g mL⁻¹, calculate the concentration of lead in ppm.
Use the relation:
ppm = mass of solute / total mass of solution × 10⁶
Also, for dilute aqueous solutions:
1 ppm ≈ 1 mg L⁻¹

solution.text: ppm = mass of solute / mass of solution × 10⁶

solution_steps: Step 1: Convert solute mass into mg
